text: You get used to running on 30% energy. I can't even remember what it feels like to not feel tired or in pain. It's a sad thing to realise and have to accept.
Nausea is a massively overlooked symptom too
Its my worse symptom
Chronic fatigue syndrome should automatically be added to your medical record once diagnosed with endo
i described it as walking through treacle
Im running on sheer willpower at this point

Fatigue was one of the worst symptoms for me, you feel like you are letting people down and people think you’re lazy. The pain is an obvious symptom but fatigue was put down to so many other issues it was really difficult to know that it was endo causing it. Anyway, after laparoscopy and the coil and a different diet, I’m feeling much better and getting to exercise and do the things I love. Don’t give up ladies xxx
I could sleep for a month and wake up exhausted. I cannot describe the tiredness in words

I’ve don’t MRI with and without contrast and nothing is found. 😔 I have severe anemia due to heavy periods, sharp pains in my chest and shoulder everytime I ovulate. Fatigued all the time, and at this point I’m not given many options. The frustration

it’s like an elevator really.. always had ups and downs but i had control most of the time.. the keyword is had.. the elevator’s cords snapped and i just keep going downwards.. sometimes it seems as if it’s slowing down, but it always returns to falling down even more.
Liz was my old Endo Nurse🥺
It’s my worst

It’s one of my worst symptoms and so debilitating. I developed endo later in life. And used to be someone who could survive of 3 hours of sleep. Now I have this condition along with insomnia and lethargy. It’s so debilitating. I was so active and now the thought of leaving the house seems too exhausting 😫
chronic fatique!
Having endo and fibromyalgia this hits hard 🥲
Having to constantly tell myself, just do this and then sit down. It’s exhausting
the feeling of you & your body not being on the same team
Fatigue, hip/leg pain has been the worst.

im willing to Take every endo Symptom only to cancel the chronic fatigue Out.
Most days im in sinc with my illness but some days...
some days i Miss myself and the Future Version of me i could have been without the Energy minimum
Stage 4 endo with bowel endo and it’s my main symptom. Only get cyclical pain for a day or two
lethargy is a symptom of many many issues in your body
my legs always feel like jelly and like they have weights on them
The fatigue is something else 💀 even when unwell too it gets worse! I’ve got stage 4 it’s awful!
Enorme misselijkheid ook.
It’s horrible how going up 10 steps can make me feel 😣 needing a 10 mins sit down break afterwards lol
Fatigue 💯 today I have sciatica, which may or may not be related
Lethargy/Fatigue is ruining my life 😭 what can I do/take to help?
vertigo
I’m forwarding this petition we need to sign it
I’m having the flare up of my life at the moment 😏 a sufferer for over 40 yrs
I have had it since I was 27 I’m 56 now
Yep💕
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/768569/signatures/new

its not even lethargy.....its absolute exhausting, debilitating exhaustion....I cant move some days...and all I want to do is sleep...I can fall asleep sat up after 2 cups of coffee after school run in a morning....its horrible xx
They obviously didn’t listen her here in Cornwall. They f*cked my case and now I have to go to Ukraine to get an operation. The country is in war and still though being better in health care.

Chronic fatigue, hives, facial flushing, heaviness , body aches ,painful sex , painful urination, painful bowel movements ,painful leg cramps bleeding and spotting blood clots big and small and when I say big as big as chicken eggs I was ignored for years by my dr ….
I always say, the day I don’t feel pain, I will be dead
The days where my eyes are rolling back, aching muscles, sleeping for hours, unable to comprehend what people are saying

gets even more lethargic when you have t1 diabetes on top with endo ,adeno pcos.arthritis .constant rashes torn hip labrums .t2 coccygeal morphology ....the list goes on . currently trying to get ready for the dentist whilst on my period and having a diabetic hypo its not even 11 am and i'm exhausted already and feel like ive run a marathon .

The lethargy is horrific and nobody understands how hard it is to deal with. People see it as me "napping" all the time, it's involuntary, once that wall hits, it's time out, I have no choice, and because endo is invisible, everyone thinks there's nothing wrong with me and no reason for it.
Fatigue is what I struggle with the most. I count myself lucky not (yet) to have the excruciating pain that others describe, but still the constant fatigue is hard to live with.

I just had a laparoscopy and they said I didn’t have endometriosis but iv seen my pictures and think I can see it also other people , is there anyone I could talk to or show these pictures any advice would be so helpful 💛💛
The fatigue is one of mine no matter how much sleep I get I feel like I haven’t slept on the odd occasion I wake up feeling great but then it soon hits again
my GP wrote on my record that my pain was minor when I said it was 9/10, have burns allll down my stomach and legs from hot water bottles etc
iam stage 4 endo tiredness is horrible even after a good night sleep endometriosis changes your whole life. I try to excuise more walking more eat well. iam currently waiting for surgery. x
But people don’t understand just how much it affects you. I know it sounds awful, I just wish people could experience it just for a day. 🥺
@Sam Swain
